instructions.instruction: **Use multiple inputs** — test with small, medium, and large inputs to catch size-dependent behavior; use realistic data sizes
instructions.original_snippets: **Use multiple inputs** — test with small, medium, and large inputs to catch size-dependent behavior; use realistic data sizes
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for planning work that should trigger benchee-profiling.

instructions.instruction: **Run sufficiently and repeatedly** — use `time: 10` (10 seconds) for reliable measurements and run 3-5 times to rule out variance
instructions.original_snippets: **Run sufficiently and repeatedly** — use `time: 10` (10 seconds) for reliable measurements and run 3-5 times to rule out variance
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for planning work that should trigger benchee-profiling.

instructions.instruction: **Identify the bottleneck** — confirm the slow call site before writing any benchmarks; if the profile output is ambiguous, re-run with a larger workload to amplify signal
instructions.original_snippets: **Identify the bottleneck** — confirm the slow call site before writing any benchmarks; if the profile output is ambiguous, re-run with a larger workload to amplify signal
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for planning work that should trigger benchee-profiling.

instructions.instruction: **Write a comparative Benchee benchmark** — implement at least 2 alternative approaches
instructions.original_snippets: **Write a comparative Benchee benchmark** — implement at least 2 alternative approaches
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for planning work that should trigger benchee-profiling.

instructions.instruction: **Save baseline** — write results to `bench/baseline.json` and check for regressions (>10% threshold); if a regression is detected, compare against the previous 3 baseline runs before raising an error to rule out noise
instructions.original_snippets: **Save baseline** — write results to `bench/baseline.json` and check for regressions (>10% threshold); if a regression is detected, compare against the previous 3 baseline runs before raising an error to rule out noise
